review_text: I enjoyed my stay here the room was very clean which is a plus with me close to shops beach hotel walking around was great I will be back.
C'était bien

Happy with the overall stay here,
Stayed in a single room cabin,
We found it was cosy, sufficient amenities, and wuite comfortable bedding.
Stayed here with minimal expectations (due to the nature of the trip and not requiring anything glamorous) but was pleasantly surprised with how good it was.
Would return and stay here again,
Only downside: the wifi didnt work for us at all.
Just love this place. Staff amazing. Very clean
The place was easy to find, neat, clean, and has friendly staff. Not too busy so it didn't feel crowded. Good place to stay and relax.
Good n clean cabin,quiet place walking distance to Flynn beach with reasonable rates
Very friendly staff, clean and comfortable rooms
It was good place very near the beach and very quiet. Seamless check in
Great park with good amenities. Cooking, pool, laundry, plenty of toilets and showers. Quiet at night and pub next door with good food. 3 min walk to beach

Amazing caravan park, friendly helpful staff and good knowledge. With good flat sites and decent size cabin too. Small but clean pool for a dip with small bbq near by. It has a decent main bbq area, with toaster kettle etc as well. Very clean amenities and decent size too. Dump point is easy to access. This also has the "local pub" next door, drop in for a good feed, beer and music afternoons.
I highly recommending coming for a stay here with the lovely local family caravan park.

We stayed one night and it was all we needed: basic and clean. I would go with kids as the triple bed can be too tiny for an adult (my kid 4yo was very happy to sleep there). I wouldn’t go for the pool. The receptionist was very helpful.

We stayed in a very clean caravan at Flynn’s Beach Caravan Park. The caravan was well maintained, with a lovely shower and a very comfortable bed – perfect after a day of exploring.

The location is truly special: surrounded by lots of greenery and birds, which creates a peaceful and natural atmosphere.

What made our stay extra memorable was the warm and caring welcome at reception. The lady there gave us so many great tips about the area. Thanks to her we visited the lighthouse with breathtaking views and did the coastal hike, which is highly recommended!

A small tip for other visitors: be aware of “brownies” (brown snakes), which are venomous. As long as you stay on the walking paths it’s not a problem, but it’s good to be aware when walking in nature.

Overall, a wonderful place to stay with friendly people, beautiful nature, and great walking opportunities.

Usamos este caravan park para una noche y no defraudó! Buenas instalaciones, justo lo que necesitabamos. Usamos el tent site y estuvo bien. Si bien la recepcion cierra muy temprano, nos dejaron el mapa con las indicaciones y nuestro nombre listo. Tiene bbq, mesas, duchas limpias y amplias, y un espacio comun para cocinar. Quizas le falta mantenimiento al area de cocina.
Stayed at the campground/caravan park at the eastern end of the park camp kitchen was handy for cooking and charging power banks and also close to the beach

My stay at Flynns Beach Caravan Park was let down by the behaviour of the owner‑manager. Instead of offering basic courtesy, they were rude when I asked simple, reasonable questions. It’s disappointing, because good customer service should be the foundation of any accommodation business. The location itself may be nice, but the attitude I encountered overshadowed the experience. I hope management improves their approach for future guests.

We found the reception staff helpful and very friendly, as was any cleaning staff we spoke with. Love the tree's for shade and the bird life that live in them.
We had a powered site, would be nice if had a concrete slab.  The amenities had great hot water. The bucket of water with mop certainly does smell by the next morning. Maybe just leave a dry mop.
Great to have The Local next door for a bite to eat n drink.
Would certainly recommend this park and if in Port Macquarie again would park our caravan.
A short walk to the beach and great coastal walks near by. Plus Sea Acres Boardwalk as must to do
Thank you for a great stay .

Camping sympa bien placé
Pas de place béton pour les camping car dommage mais si non très bien
Douches très propres
Personnel sympa

We stayed in the 3-bed family cabin for one night, cabin was fairly clean and park is quiet with friendly receptionists. A/C worked well, we assume that they are in the middle of renovations etc however there are a few things that could be improved. ||- WIFI was basically useless. |- Bathroom had mould growing on roof.|- No bathroom bin provided so we made our own.  (inconvenient for those who need to dispose sanitary items) |-  Clothesline provided but no pegs?.|-  Exhaust Fan cover in kitchen had been removed leaving open wires etc |- Kitchen was missing a drawer. ||Overall, good pit stop but just needs that extra love.

Great location close to beaches, local pub & small shopping centre.

Very reasonably priced with immaculate facilities & pool.

I would return here if looking to stay in this area again

We stayed at Flynns Beach Caravan Park on the way back from a trip to the Gold Coast, for two nights. We paid well over $500 for the beach house accommodation, and was bitterly disappointed.
The cabin we rented was one of the oldest in the park, the photos on their website were terribly docked, and looked far nicer on the website than in real life.
The railing up the stairs was broken, my daughter nearly fell through this on the first afternoon we arrived, and the stairs were rickety. Inside fairly clean, however the shower screen was rusted in place in the bathroom, and a shower curtain was the replacement. Beds were hard, and makeshift...timber bed base made out of offcuts, and a strange mattress with springs felt through at times.
Advertised WIFI but in actual fact in the beach house, this did not work at all.
On the whole, felt ripped off with cost, we did travel over the long weekend, so I understand prices are inflated, but even at a cheaper cost, still would not stay again.

Lovely place. Very basic but we stayed in the larger holiday home that was self contained. Close to the beach. We loved the surl life saving club on Flynn's beach for breakfast everyday ! Excellent coastal walks as well.
Great place and clean kid friendly walking distance to beach, bottle shop and restaurant  and tavern 🍺 you can book 12 months in advance now so get in and pick the best spot .

Fint campsite til en enkelt eller 2 overnatninger, da det har god placering ift. byen.
Dog et lille campsite med en lille pool.
Køkken faciliteterne var middelmådige og toiletterne var rene og pæne.
Et sted hvor det virker til at nogle bor halv permanent, hvilket gør at nogle af Campsites er lidt slidte og ikke særlig velholdt.
Meget roligt og venlige gæster.
Nice location. Camp kitchen was pretty poor.

The park is well located just a 5 minute walk to Flynn’s Beach however we stayed in the Beach House which clearly needed updating and there were several outstanding maintenance issues. Unfortunately way over priced for the condition the cabin was in.
We love our stay there very much. We got a very clean and nice carbin. It’s within walking distance from the  beautiful Flynns Beach. The price’s affordable too. We will come back soon.
A quiet caravan / camping ground in a lovely treed environment with Kookaburras. A stones throw fom the beach and beach side cafe strip. Sports bar next door.
Frauen Toiletten waren alle dreckig
Great location good value . for caravaning

The receptionist was friendly and kind. However, the cabins are really not worth the money. They are clean, but for $160 per night, found wanting. The cabin smelt quite bad, there is no cleaning service, so you have the same towels for the duration of your stay. The bed was extremely uncomfortable and very small. None of the curtains close entirely so there is always sunlight coming in in the morning. Apart from the bed, these are all little things, but they added up to mean it wasn't worth $160 a night in my opinion.
Superbe emplacement
The cabin we stayed in was a bit dated but clean and comfy
Clean well located cabin. Five minutes walk to the beach. Comfortable bed.

We did a 5 week trip up the East Coast and this was one of our favourite campsites. The cooking facilities were clean. The bathrooms were modern and clean, also were cleaned throughout the day. The staff were great over the phone and being a late arrival was totally stress free. The camp site is also a short walk away from a lovely beach. Would definitely recommend!

To be honest we didn’t get to stay at Flynn’s because of a booking mistake which was totally my fault. The lovely man at reception couldn’t offer my family accommodation for the night due to being booked out but he did refund the cost of the accommodation which he didn’t have to do. It was much appreciated and we will be back hopefully with the right dates next time.
Clean amenities, staff were lovely, shady camp site and within walking distance of the beach :)

Die Camp kitchen und Sanitären Anlagen waren gut aber der Stellplatz den ich hatte sehr schlecht.  An der Ecke zur Straße und hinter einem Pub der bis 22:00 Uhr geöffnet war und dessen Lüftung sehr laut war. Dazu am Durchgang zum Pub und der Ortschaft
Nice shady area's, has tennis courts small play area and pool. Nice showers.
Just a little different to park large ute in reverse

A Fantastic, Stress-Free Stay!

We just spent a wonderful Friday here. The caravan park is very easy to access. The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful. Best of all, the amenities block was clean & well maintained. Overall value for money.

This place was great. Studio cabins were clean, and well equipped. The person at the reception was also very helpful. The caravan park is located near the beach and well located for a nice walk around the place
Nice place, it's got everything you need!!!

Das von mir angemietete Häuschen war sehr praktisch und funktionell eingerichtet. Mit eigener Küchenzeile und Dusche/WC
ist man auch unabhängig von den Waschhäusern auf dem Campingplatz. Das Team ist sehr freundlich und hilfsbereit und man hat um die Ecke an der Küstenstraße einige Einkaufsmöglichkeiten. Angrenzend am Campingplatz ist die Flynns Beach Bar und die ist einfach super! Zum Pazifik-Strand sind es gerade mal 3 - 4 Gehminuten.
Great place service excellent
Nice but was a bit far to walk for us to Flynn's beach..

It’s ok here and the location is pretty central in Port Macquarie but it’s not on the beach as the name kind of suggests. |Our site was handy to the pool and also to the amenities which was useful. We were on a grass slope with a gravel patch but the site was drive through and access was simple enough. |It had been raining before we arrived so everything was damp on the grass. Other sites in the park were just mud so we were better off than some. |We didn’t use the camp kitchen as it was a long way from our site but it looked fine.

Adequate but very average. Not actually on the beach- nor within easy walking distance. After rain the attractive end of the park is a bog so you are left with a gravel sprinkled grass site- no trees or foliage. WiFi doesn’t work and there’s no TV service.

After recently staying in the beach house for 4 nights we do not recommend.... we are regulars and will not be returning.. the kitchen was so grubby with grease and on top of all kitchen cupboard was piles of cockroach or mice droppings(havnt been cleaned for quite some time... there was only 2 of us in a 4 birth house and hot water would run out in 2 mins so one of us was showering in the public amenities block ...the air con was sooo loud and old could barely hear the TV,  the kitchen table almost collapsed when eating dinner...the lounge felt dirty to sit on so we got a sheet off the spare bed to put on the lounge and and lounge also had small holes in it...the toilet blind was very mouldy and when we pulled the kitchen blind down of an evening something had been thrown at it (liquid) and had ran down to the bottom of it... the WiFi was terribl and made it sooo frustrating and couldn't watch any movies..the sad part was both owners drive a luxury range rover each' and upon checking out and telling the actual owner about a couple of these things she didn't really seem Interested and just said oh sorry...that's why I write this lengthy review!!!
Great staff. Great location away from bussle of town. 5 mins walk to either dog beach or Flynn's beach.  Cafes and chemist and medical close by. Great location for walking trails.
Perfect
The staff member i spoke to was so welcoming, warm, and friendly. The room was ok and it was clean. We had one of the cheaper villas. Would recommend the dearer ones.

Nos hemos quedado en una parcela sin electricidad. Buen tamaño y bien mantenida. Baños y duchas totalmente nuevos, todo muy limpio. Cocina al aire libre con lo necesario para cocinar aunque algunos electrodomésticos necesitan ser reemplazados. Muy tranquilo y sin ruidos. Cerca de la playa para pasear.
環境非常優美

A lovely place in bush settings close to beach and port Macquarie
Quite and relaxing .
Beautiful little family run park, neat and tidy and very well maintained. Had a nice couple of nights
Clean and tidy cabins, basic but nice. Hot water ran out quickly but came back on again after a few hours. Good location, friendly staff
Nice little caravan park handy to local shopping area
Cabin very clean and tidy

Recently I had to cancel my reservation with Flynns Beach and the staff were so helpful, I just had to let the world know.
Thank you to the team at Flynns Beach Caravan Park 🙏🏻.  I appreciate your kindness and professionalism.

We stayed in a small one room cabin with kitchenette.
Shower was hot and strong
Bed was very comfy
Location perfect
Very quiet
Beautiful foliage around our cabin offered privacy.
But cabin definitely needed an upgrade to its steps and porch and window furnishings. Toilet roll holder was all rusted - not a good look.

Its not as it looks on pictures. Cabin so dirty and unhygienic. Not a best place for kids if u opt cabins. Though we booked for 4nights. Last 2 nights we stayed at another accommodation. Been in many caravan park cabins but this is the worst one. Everywhere ants. Beds are uncomfortable. Nothing best to describe apart from visitors 🦜 🦜 in the mrg

DO NOT BOOK THE CABINS. Horrible smell coming from our room. Could not get it out even with the doors/windows open and kitchen fan on. Was so bad almost just considered leaving that night. Very musty in room and dirty cover left on couch. Rust on towel racks and peeling side boards. How cheap to not even replace these things. Possible mould under the fridge door. Only a small measly bar of soap provided. Thankfully the bed linen was clean but cabins need a huge refresh. Not worth the $$. Close to beach.
Cheap and cheerful. Good place for an overnighter
Very cool, neat and simple. The problem was that the Camp Kitchen does not have a fridge or freezer.

Good rooms, great location.
The pools have solar heating, with the main one having a gas booster, but both were still cold.
Clean amenities. Friendly staff, next to pub close proximity to beach what more could ask for when looking for a place to camp
Amy and the whole team were fantastic.  Friendly,  professional and super accomdating. After hours arrival was made super easy. Close to everything.  Mokopuna and I DEF booking longer next time!

We contacted Flynns Beach Caravan Park requesting a disabled friendly cabin, we were told they did not have disabled cabins but they would give us one which is manageable for a disabled person......
We were shocked to find the door openings into the bathroom and bedroom measures 570mm our STANDARD size wheelie walking frame is 600mm wide...
So getting into the bathroom and bedroom I had to support my wife by hand as she very slowly struggled to walk, likewise when she needed to use the toilet during the night I had to again support her weight and slowly manouver her to and from the toilet.
The bathroom which includes the shower and toilet was tiny and thankfully we only stayed two nights as both were not disabled friendly.

The TV was a cheap Chinese brand $200 Falcon which constantly lost signal making it unwatchable.
I contacted the site manager who came to look at the TV when it had no signal and suggested I try tuning the channels in, and then said when it's windy the antenna's lose signal, there was little or no wind blowing when he said this.
The fridge again a cheap Chinese Chiq $300 which was new so worked ok....

Now my main complaint the cabin looks to be new and another new one was being constructed right next door, so the noise during the day meant if the cabin was comfortable to watch TV in ( which it wasn't) we had to go out to spend the day elsewhere.
There was evidence on.the site that the two previous cabins had burnt down as there was the charred remains of a burnt out AC unit nest to our cabin, which leads me to think was that Chinese brand A.C.

The interior walls in the cabin is nothing more substantial than cardboard as I could literally push on them and they were flexing.

The only positive thing I can say is the new AC in the new cabin was a Mitsubishi a known quality brand....

I cannot comment on what it would be like using this park if you had a caravan and don't know what the public amenities are like...

In summarising I would not recommend staying in a cabin at Flynns Beach Caravan Park as it is the worse experience we've ever encountered having stayed in many other disabled friendly cabins.

Noel Walsh.
Very nice caravan park for a family to stay close to beach and shops
Had to cancel not even 10 hrs after booking still 48 hr notice but no let's keep $160 for cancellation fee discusting
Sehr gutes WLAN auf dem Campingplatz. Nahe am Strand gelegen. Der Weg kann locker zu Fuss zurückgelegt werden.
Just didn’t feel safe as anyone can access it. No security gate or anything similar.  Showers and toilets were very modern and clean

We really liked this place. Very comfortable little cabin which was cheap and cheerful- very clean and great comfortable linens. Walking distance to amazing Flynn Beach and next door to fantastic restaurant/ bar called The Local. Would definitely stay there again.

Lovely quiet and safe camping site. Has a friendly laid back feel. Lots of trees and birds. Amenities are great, close to the beach and a local bar next door if the mood takes you. I would definitely recommend this park especially if you want to avoid the ‘big’ parks.
Best caravan park! So close to the beach only a 5min walk. Also the reception staff are amazing helped us with our car troubles :))
Booked there for a week whole time wifi useless pay top dollar and no internet and no tv as wifi useless

Really enjoyed our stay here. Very peaceful and relaxing and felt very rainforest-y. There is a sports bar next door that had live music each night which gave the park a good vibe! Good location, close to the beach and surf clubs. Would stay again ☺️
Well I didn't get to stay here, I rang twice, but no one had the common courtesy to ring me back, very unprofessional, I can see why it 3 star rating.

Great little park. Away from the busy roads. Great location; close to beaches, cafes and bars. Lots of shade at the tent sites, surrounded by establish trees, jungle like plants and well maintained lawns. Probably our favourite park on this adventure! The staff were lovely also.

Small quiet, large shady sites in a quiet area of Port Macquarie.|Spotless amenities with small swimming pool & BBQ area. Nice camp kitchen.|10 min walk to patrolled surf beach & 25 mins into town.|A Sports Bar is next door with live music. Was great entertainment.|Highly recommend.
Beautiful caravan park! Stopped in for one night on our way south. Lovely beach meets the bush. Amenities were nice and clean. Highly recommend and wish we could have stayed longer!
Unpowered sites are great. Ample camp kitchen but no fridge. Amazing spot at Flynn’s beach SLSC and The Local pub

Stayed in Powered Site. Clean, large & shady site.  Close to camp kitchen & amenities. 10 min walk to Flynn's Beach & 20 mins into town Beach.
Quiet camp ground however is next door to a Sports Bar, but they had great music that finished by 9pm
Highly recommend

We caravan Australia full time and love this park and stay here every time we are in Port Macquarie. They are such a good location, across the road from one of the best beaches with cafes etc, coastal walking tracks and just a short 5-10min drive to the centre of town (or easy coastal walk). The staff are so friendly and helpful and everything is kept clean and tidy. Our kids rate this park highly and they have been everywhere! Thanks Aimee, Ian and all the staff. 🤙🏼🌞

Careful booking here. They will take your money and not return it if you cancel. I booked for NEXT year. Changed plans and cancelled within days and well with cancellation time frame. This was a month ago now. They have admitted they owe me money but REFUSE to refund it until I take down my review. IAN emailed calling me a coward for refusing to be bribed to have my money returned. Lodged a complaint with Dept of Fair Trading and have lodged with the ACCC today. My bank is also now involved. All emails acknowledging they owe money have been forwarded to relevant parties as have IAN’S emails name calling me because I am asking for me money back. DO NOT book here. Happy to provide proof of my interactions to prove this is factual.

Only stayed o/night was pretty quiet when we stayed.
We parked our van out front and locked it up to go look around Port for the day. Quite a lot of caravaners do it a there.
Highly recommended! Comfortable, clean, peaceful, and surrounded by a beautiful natural setting.

We spent 3 nights on a powered site.  Awesome location, surrounded by lovely tree's and beautiful beaches all within walking distance.  We loved being able to walk down to the beach with the kids and enjoying the birds in the morning (not to mention the koala living in the park).  Great friendly staff also. Thanks guys.

Took my money and will not give the deposit back although we are well within the cancellation period. Will not respond to emails about refund. I have tried two different time. Can’t get anyone to answer the phone or get told they will have to pass on details. Assuming this doesn’t happen or they are just ignoring me, hoping I will forget about my $150. Have had to contact my bank and Dept of Fair Trading for help.

This was a really Nice Stay|This was the perfect spot for my kids and I. Had a great size boathouse with full amenities, comfortable bed, pool on premises, small grass space all laundry facilities. It was a nice stay, just out of town but only few minutes drive. Staff were friendly upon arrival and was helpful and welcoming.
Good location, cheap average rooms, showers and water terrible.

Made very easy to book on the afternoon for our late arrival (same day). Helpful and friendly. Beautifully appointed amenities, clearly of a high standard, cleaned and excellently maintained. Picturesque. Adopter walk to Flynn’s Beach and a little Local Bar nearby. Definitely spend more time here next time, sadly this was a quick night stop over. Thank you for your pride and delightful park.

Very disappointed with the cabins. Stayed 2 nights in cabin 12 & 2 nights in cabin 15. We were hoping that the second cabin would have been better, but a HUGE no.  They need a massive overhaul. At $150 per night, extremely expensive for what these are.  Old cutlery, old rusted towel rail in bathroom, had to hold foot against bathroom door to stop it hitting you in the back whilst at the bathroom sink, bedspread hasn’t been updated sine 1970, no washing up brush, a quarter of a green kitchen scourer, (what the???), cheapest toilet paper.  Cheap plastic dining chairs & kitchen knife wouldn’t even cut a tomato! Husband took knife out & sharpened it on a cement slab.  Forget about watching TV.  It was so pixelated because of the wind (nothing you can do about the wind), & mobile reception pretty ordinary. Even if these cabins were $50 a night, I’d would not stay here.  On a positive note, great location.

Floor felt sandy like it had not been vacuumed/swept throughly after the last guests. The dishrack was rusty so we didn't want to use it. The non-stick pot provided to cook was clearly scratched, which means it becomes toxic to use. The green scourer provided to wash the dishes with was quite hard to use and can also be abrasive and scratch the non-stick coating on pots and pans.Other than those minor things, the standard cabin was spacious and the triple bunk bed seems fun if you have kids.
Such a lovely quiet caravan park ✨️

Cabin totally NOT what the photos on booking.com or the website look like. We were shocked to walk into the cabin. As budget as it gets (for $200p/n)|1 small lounge for two people, a tiny table with 4 plastic chairs, curtains didn’t close properly, no window in the kitchen. The double bed was so uncomfortable and springy that every time you moved it made horrible noises. Bunks were vinyl covered thin mattresses, no fan in that room. |We all felt like the cabin was on a tilt and guess what, when we laid a water bottle on its side it quickly rolled across the room. |Only stayed 1 night, in fact we stayed only 11hrs. |Very very disappointed.

Stayed in a basic on-site cabin. Space was limited, it had an odd assortment of crockery, glassware and cutlery and though meant for two it had only a single chair and table on the small entry porch. Location-wise it's within walking distance of Flynn's Beach itself but you'd need to drive to the CBD. Our cabin was also right next to a walkway to the amenities block with folks passing by the window every few minutes. Still, you get what you pay for.

Stayed there last week. Was great stay.
Friendly staff.. Amenities clean
Kitchen wonderful.
Wildlife interesting😊 Location to beach great
Thank you
Wonderful clean caravan park.

UNSAFE CARAVAN/TENT SITE. We arrived after hours & noticed there were trees all around the site including a big gumtree which are known for falling over. If the gumtree falls, it would fall directly onto somebody’s tent or caravan. There was also lots of branches directly over the site. We didn’t want to put our kids at risk so we ended up leaving. No one should be aloud to stay on the site, please check if your site has a gumtree on it before booking.

Trip will book for kids but the park didn't receive this information so we were short 3 sets of linen and towels. Overall okay. Small family run business so when requested towels and sheets took over an hour to drop off.  The hot water system sign was missing which should have told us to wait 10 min after each shower so we all had cold showers.

Small caravan park in a beautiful part of Port Macquarie. Stayed in the 2 bedroom Palm House and it was fantastic: spacious and modern with everything that you need. The park itself has basic amenities with a small pool. Staff were very friendly and check-in was a breeze. Wouldn’t hesitate staying there again next time I visit the area.

We visited Flynns beach in a camper back in March this year.|We had an amazing time. The facilities were immaculate. |It was so nice to see all the birds and wildlife around the area. |The location was also very good. |We would definitely stay again in the future!

We stayed for a night at an unpowered site. It was alright but very close to the street so there was a bit of noise. Amenities were good, but please clean the air vents over the women’s showers🙏🏼 they all have a thick layer of dust.

45$ pro Nacht in der Nebensaison ist einfach zu teuer.
Die Küche war dreckig und das Wlan war sehr schlecht.
Stellplatz ansonsten war okey, die Waschräume waren sehr sauber und gepflegt.

The room was clean, but I saw a small cockroach inside my spoon drawer. Location was okay.
The instruments were old, the toaster was rusty.
Flynns beach Beach hut cabin. Unit is older but well maintained and was clean on arrival. Internet slow and disconnects Bed comfy. Good spot to stay.
Nice place to explore port macquarie

Pretty small and dated cabins, could definitely do with some tlc and a little upgrade, i walked in the bathroom and in one spot where i stepped it felt like my heal went through the floor, upon looking it seemed the floor is a bit damaged there. Bed was uncomfortable, could feel and hear every spring when moving in bed, it was obvious the matress was quite old. Windows were filthy, I wouldn't have them open because they're so dirty and have a lot of webs etc. My partner and I both didn't feel comfortable showering in the shower because of its size and the shower curtain.

Cabin overall seemed clean but I felt like it was steep to charge $130 p/n not only for a small outdated cabin with no face washers, no hand towels, no tissues, no hairdryer, i was looking for the scourer and found a small slither, not even a third of a scourer!  then told to clean up after myself and take out all rubbish, dry and put dishes away etc. I leave things clean usually but tend to leave rubbish, this statement made be a little nervous as it seems like they want the cabins all cleaned by guests so their cleaners don't have to do much if anything. I thought is my cleaning up to their standard or will they still charge a cleaning fee.
I wouldn't stay here again and would suggest to stay elsewhere when visiting Port Macquarie.
Only one set of amenities makes it awkward.

Absolute mud hole. Do not camp there. Their saving grace was their new toilet block. The staff were nice but not organised. The camp "kitchen" was near pointless, a gas mate stove and an umbrella would be an equivalent.
Doing a lap of Australia and this unfortunately has been our most regretful stay.

We booked for a week. The rain turned all the van sites into mud. No concrete or gravel to site ones van, but uneven mud pits. The new shower block is fabulous, but there's no path. One must walk through mud &  soggy lawn first. Obviously I do not hold the Park responsible for the weather, but visitors expect the park to cater for human use & not drag mud into the van, the car, the showers etc.
Oh, and let's never. Ever. Forget the ants.  Nests everywhere. Until we were able to buy ant killer in bulk, they also got into our van. We didn't stay the whole week but fled. A shame, bc the location is superb.
What a  terrific family-friendly caravan park. I have been there on many occasions and every time it has been enjoyable. Awesome staff too.
Caravan sites  are well maintained and spacious. Helpful staff, friendly and accommodating.  Watch out for the bush turkeys!!

We’ve stay in some pretty ordinary accommodation but this takes the cake
Where do I start the floor was dirty from the previous occupants. I felt uncomfortable letting my daughter crawl around on the floor, I asked for a cot to be supplied lucky I brought our own otherwise she would have had no where to sleep I’m sure the cot was included in the high price we paid per night.
I arranged for a late check in as we didn’t arrive until 7pm reception closes at 6pm so couldn’t even question it.
The bathroom was tiny there were no hand towels or face washers.
The audacity of the owners to put signs up to say this is an unserviced unit and clean up after yourselves if not you would be charged a cleaning fee.
I’ve paid less for an air bnb in the area that’s twice the size think I’ll stick with them.

Had 3 nights here in our van as there is so much to do and see in Port Maquarie and this camp is well located. There is also a lovely beach a short stroll away. The pitches next to the pub are noisy to be fair but also handy for a quick beer 😁 The Koala hospital is round the corner and is awesome.
Awesome park great fresh amenities and pool. Management and staff are friendly and accommodating. Recommend to anyone!

Camped overnight in my van and did it again because the place is convenient,
The service is friendly, price is right and layout of camp welcoming.
The bathrooms are also clean for vanners.

We stayed for ten days. Great location to use as a base to discover Port Macquarie and surrounds. Dog friendly with the local dog beach in walking distance. Facilities were modern, clean and cared for. Ask for a quiet spot as there is a pub on the coast side of the park and that goes off on the weekends. It rained when we were there but the drainage is well planned and we didn't have any issues. Washing is coin operated with plenty of hanging space. Would recommend this site.

I travelled all the way from Western Australia to visit Ill family and stayed in a standard double cabin here with my sister. 2 nights for $300. The staff were polite yet disinterested. The cabins could do with a bit of an upgrade, and I ended up munched on by bed bugs. Spacious enough and in a convenient location.
Powered site, plenty of room and good location.
Only overnight stay but was good

Sorry to say I cannot recommend this place.  The 2 bedroom cabin was tiny, old and very tired.  The pretty blue and white cabins with light and airy interiors on the website were nothing like the cabin we were allocated. Our "standard" cabin, compared with similarly described cabins in the many others in which we have stayed, was anything but "standard" : shabby and dingy.
Both the shower recess and bathroom were so tiny they were almost impossible to move in.  The bathroom had a notice stating "by necessity these cabins have a relatively small hot water service. Please keep your showers to a minimum time and temperature and wait approx 15 minutes between showers to avoid the hot water running out.  Sorry for any inconvenience"!  (The cabin is supposed to accommodate up to 5 people.)
There was no shampoo, conditioner, hairdryer, hand towels or iron. The curtains had holes in them, with broken hanging clips and the white linings were mouldy. The dining chairs were the cheapest, flimsy plastic.  Instead of a full pot scourer, we were provided with one-third of one! The dirty couch with elasticised cover caused much sneezing and coughing when sat upon. The hanging rails in the cupboards were situated so close to the shelf above it was nigh on impossible to hang coat hangers without scraping fingers and the cupboards were too narrow to take hanging clothes. The ceiling above the couch had brown stains on it.

We had a powered camp site, which was a generous size, and in a peaceful corner of the park. The facilities were modern and clean with a laundry to clean your clothes. It is a coin laundry, you can get all the change you need at the office. The staff were fantastic and guided us to all of the best local places to go. The park has a lovely atmosphere and was very well priced.
Beautiful park . Quiet clean safe. 👌

Fantastic location, Just a few minutes walk to access number of beaches
The rate was reasonable, stayed overnight unpowered site for $35
The kitchen has a couple of gas stoves and a kettle, but no microwave and fridge
Great caravanpark. Swimmingpool. Very clean and new toilets and showers. Just great!
Great little place to stay.  Cabins were comfy and clean.

Exceptional location (stones throw from the cafe on the cliff). Facilities were pleasant, newly renovated amenities. Great campsite with serene nature surroundings. Staff were incredibly accommodating, friendly and flexible when changing dates. Would strongly recommend.

6 mths ago.Very friendly, helpful and accommodating staff. Well kept tidy park . Our cabin was clean well appointed for our stay but very very tired and in need of refurbishing. The floor in the bathroom is suffering from years of moisture. Air conditioner worked well, and the linen was spotless.
Today
Stayed in a single room cabin this time. Plenty of space. Full sized fridge and kitchenette. Heaps better than a motel room. Comfy bed. Clean. No neighbours keeping you awake and no noise from 10.00 pm being in a caravan park. Heaps cheaper than a motel but so much better in my opinion, surrounded by birds and trees.

We stayed in the beachhouse for 4 nights, couldn't fault it at all. Had everything we could possibly need. Staff were great, check in and check out were easy. Kids loved the pool. They don't service the house but we went and asked for more towels on the 3rd day and they were more than willing to change our towels. Would definitely stay again.
Close to public buses, beach right at the end of the street, quiet relaxing friendly.

Absolutely atrocious. Wifi doesn’t work, water barely works, staff are terrible, extremely overpriced and upselled, $400 a night for half the lights and fans to not work (this is in the beach house which is the most expensive and “comfortable” cabin). DO NOT recommend staying here unless booking a camping ground. Horrible service and a waste of money.

We’ve been camping here for two years and we’ve booked our third year for this Xmas.  Cannot fault this caravan park the staff are so friendly and helpful the grounds are cleaned daily, the amenities are clean and it’s just a pretty place to stay. Close to the beach, bar and shops.  Perfect spot.

Unfortunately, we would not recommend the Beach Room.  It cost us $445.00 for one night.  The whole house needs an upgrade especially the bathroom.  The House is positioned near the pool, tents and caravans.  For that price you would want to be in a more secluded, quiet area of the park.  The House was quirky but it was dated back to the 1970's.  We would have liked something more comfortable and upmarket for that price.
Sehr großer sauberer Strand, viele kostenlose Parkplätze. Toiletten und Duschen vorhanden.
we had a great stay, a shame the koalas kept us awake at night ;-)
Advertised as a 4 star park, I would not recommend it to anyone who wants a clean cabin if it decided to rain as all access is dirt, the cabin we stayed in was to say the least poor
Cozy cabin and clean
Unpowered  sites are all under trees so very little solar power input  otherwise ok
Nice park close to the beach and shops. Brand new amenities. Camp kitchen was very basic it could use a kettle

Good budget accommodation. Fridge and clothes washer could be better, IMHO. Washer has evidently seen some abuse from past guests and the fridge was a bit underpowered. Aircon and fans all work effectively, clean and tidy. Firm but comfortable beds, clean linen and towels provided.
The new amenities are very schmick.

We booked two cabins for the weekend and were left very disappointed.

When we booked we were not made aware of the upgrades that would be going on during our stay, or of the fact that the park would not have any communal toilets or showers available during this period.

on the Saturday of our stay, there was an asbestos removal team removing the asbestos sheeting from the old toilet block directly next to the pool and communal BBQ area, when we went to use the night before found the gas bottle empty and had to hunt around and find one as the office was shut. The next day Saturday, there was an asbestos removal team removing the asbestos sheeting from the old toilet block directly next to the pool.

The villas were also a little below par for a park, the main bedroom in one of the units had a light globe hanging from the ceiling fan that was only operational when the light was turned on. the other room in this villa had a fan with two speeds gale force or hurricane and paint flaking off the external ceilings.

The one true shining light from the weekend was the friendly birdlife that would join the kids for their meals and let them hand-feed them.

This park was great to stay at, we felt very safe here.
Lovely atmosphere, lots of trees and bush turkeys!
Nice short stroll to the beach (which is stunning) restaurant next door and a cafe on the beach.
There is a swimming pool which is good.
Cabins are simple, quite old, screens and sills were dirty, some window locks were broken.
The screen door to our cabin did not lock at all.
The floor in the bathroom has a very weak spot that if you accidentally tred in it, feels like it will fall through.
Kitchen was fine for basics (microwave, fridge, pans, crockery, cutlery) however if you are here for more than one night be aware that the cabins are not serviced so no tea coffee sugar top ups and no clean towels other than the ones on arrival.

Definitely happy with our stay. Stayed 3 nights and we’re happy and would definitely return. Bed comfort was fine . Kitchen had cooking facilities which were ok although there is only hot plate and microwave available. Shower stated to have shorter showers due to hot water issues. We didn’t experience a drama. As stat  ex we would definitely return and stay again. Calue for money based on costs paid

Receptionist was very friendly but the cabin was bad. The basin in the bathroom was super tiny and the mattress in the 2nd bedroom (bunk beds) had a plastic cover which was very uncomfortable. There is also a warning message for guests on the dining table, stating that you will be charged $ 50 if you leave towels on the floor, don't clean the cabin and take out the trash before leaving. I found this very inappropriate and insulting and I have never seen anything like that in another caravan park! Would not book there again!!!
Great Location & Price. Room was a bit smaller than we thought. It was good for a short stay.
Great, well kept park. Friendly staff. Roomy van sites. Gives impression of counrty retreat. Are self contained, but note new amenity block under construction. Highly recommended.
Beautiful bushy caravan park can't wait to go back

The cabin we stayed in was great, if you were being picky hole from previous blinds fixtures had not been repaired and kitchen missing a blind altogether. But lots of space and plenty of beds, car port could fit 2 cars (1 large 1 small) front to back, washing machine very handy and air con in main area and main bedroom. Pool not the biggest, but beach not to far away.
We camped, but this is a really nice place to stay

Camped at Flynn’s Beach Caravan Park over the Easter long weekend amongst the beautiful trees & singing birds. Nice little park & welcoming staff. Close to town & all the places to see & do while visiting Port Macquarie. Short walk to flynns beach from the park.

Been coming here during my yearly stay in Port Mac for years now, and I have to say that I really enjoy staying at the caravan park with my family. Staff are very welcoming and friendly, and the rooms are always kept neat and tidy. The only thing that could improve is stronger Wi-Fi coverage, but that's about it. Would recommend to stay here :)
It is fairly a good place to stay with caravan parking area. Comfortable
We stayed on a caravan site for the night and the staff were very friendly and helpful
Great place to stay, and 5 minutes from the water front

Stayed in the Beach House, which was nice but a bit old. Nothing wrong specifically but nothing special. Location of the caravan park was good but surrounded by campers that love waking up early and packing down (sounds like they are in your cabin). Didn’t see much of the rest of the park however.
There was a washing machine provided but no clothes line or even a clothes horse which was a bit disappointing.
Wonderful  few  days spent  there

Very good ! Juste no fridge in the camp kitchen :)
30$ for 2 people in tent
